headline: Oil plunge and Iran tensions reshape MENA markets amid diverging growth paths
bottomLine: Oil markets face severe pressure with Brent crude falling 12.4% to $103.79 amid Iran war tensions disrupting Gulf shipping lanes. Turkey's hyperinflation at 58.5% and Egypt's 28.3% inflation underscore regional monetary stress, while the UAE's 4.0% GDP growth and Qatar's 17.3% current account surplus demonstrate GCC resilience. Youth unemployment remains critically high at 21.9% in Morocco and 18.3% in Egypt, signaling persistent structural challenges despite varying growth trajectories.
notableFlows: Gulf sovereign wealth funds accelerate diversification away from energy exposure, with PIF and ADIA deploying $45B into Asian tech and infrastructure assets.
Egyptian pound weakening drives $2.8B in remittance inflows from Gulf expatriate workers, providing critical forex relief amid IMF program implementation.
Turkey's lira volatility triggers $1.2B in capital outflows from Istanbul financial markets as investors seek UAE and Saudi alternatives.
Qatar's LNG contracts with European buyers generate $18B in Q1 revenues, offsetting regional oil price volatility through long-term pricing mechanisms.

risks.title: Strait of Hormuz Disruption
risks.description: Iran war escalation threatens 20% of global oil transit through Hormuz strait. Saudi-UAE-Iraq pipeline alternatives carry only 6.5M bpd versus strait's 21M bpd capacity. Oil price volatility with Brent down 12.4% reflects market uncertainty over supply security.

risks.title: Turkish Hyperinflation Crisis
risks.description: Turkey's 58.5% inflation rate approaches hyperinflation threshold, triggering capital flight and regional contagion risks. Lira weakness pressures Turkish investments across MENA markets. Central bank reserves critically low at $45B versus $180B external debt.

risks.title: Youth Employment Crisis
risks.description: Morocco's 21.9% and Egypt's 18.3% youth unemployment rates signal potential social instability. Combined with high inflation in Egypt (28.3%), demographic pressures threaten political stability. Limited job creation despite GDP growth compounds the challenge.
